channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump administration deports hundreds of migrants even as judge orders that removals be stopped
item.description: The Trump administration has transferred hundreds of immigrants to El Salvador even as a federal judge issued an order temporarily barring the deportations under an 18th century wartime declaration targeting Venezuelan gang members.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal judge blocks Trump's plan to target 'alien enemies' for deportation
item.description: Trump invoked the wartime authority Alien Enemies Act, targeting members of Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua for expedited deportation. But a federal judge blocked the action Saturday evening.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: DOGE's plans to offload government buildings supported by former GSA official
item.description: Former GSA Administrator Emily Murphy told Fox News Digital that the GSA will “rightsize its portfolio" by selling or leasing unused government buildings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump watches strike on Iran-backed Houthis in Yemen in new White House pics as large-scale op continues
item.description: The White House released new photos of President Donald Trump watching the large-scale U.S. operation striking Iran-backed Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration ramps up rhetoric targeting the courts amid mounting legal setbacks
item.description: President Donald Trump’s supporters are echoing some of the rhetoric and actions that elsewhere have preceded attacks on the judiciary as the courts deliver a series of setbacks to his dramatic attempt to change the federal government without congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Angst pervades a pair of Republican town halls
item.description: In two congressional districts and vastly different political environments, two Republicans were met with far different reactions at public meetings they held late last week.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum, Makiya Seminera And Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tennessee bill allows schools to deny enrollment for illegal migrants, proposal panned as unconstitutional
item.description: Tennessee state lawmakers introduced a bill to allow school districts and law enforcement agencies to deny enrollment to illegal migrant students.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump invokes wartime Alien Enemies Act of 1798 to target violent illegal immigrant street gangs
item.description: President Donald Trump invoked an 18th century law, used only during the War of 1812, World War I and World War II Saturday to curb illegal immigrant gangs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sec. Noem reports reduced border crossings, aggressive deportation efforts
item.description: The secretary visited Nogales, Arizona, on Saturday to assess the border situation firsthand as illegal crossings hit their lowest levels.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: VA denies policy change after internal memo rescinds transgender veterans’ guidance
item.description: In an internal VA memo seen by NPR, the VA says it's rescinding a directive that contains detailed guidance on the kind of care transgender veterans can receive at VA facilities.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs a bill funding the government for 6 months, avoiding a shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has signed into law legislation funding the government through the end of September, ending the threat of a partial government shutdown and capping off a struggle in Congress that deeply divided Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China's US influence could face crackdown under slate of new bills
item.description: U.S. Sen. James Lankford, R-Okla., rolled out several bills aimed at countering Chinese influence and ownership in the U.S. as tensions grow.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats confront limits of their minority power after bruising shutdown vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Democrats were grim Friday as they left Washington after a brutal 10-week stretch that consistently showed the limits of their power in the minority — and culminated with a deeply personal rupture over how to best counter President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump undoes stack of 'harmful' Biden executive orders, shrinks multiple agencies
item.description: The White House on Friday said President Donald Trump took more actions to downsize the federal government through several executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Senate approves funding bill hours before shutdown deadline, sending to Trump for signature
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate passed a Republican-led spending bill Friday hours before a government shutdown, overcoming sharp Democratic opposition to the measure and sending it to President Donald Trump to be signed into law.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma schools chief won’t abandon classroom Bible effort
item.description: Last year, Ryan Walters issued a mandate for schools to incorporate lessons on the Bible in grades 5 through 12.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Grenell developing 'common sense' plan to turn Kennedy Center financials around
item.description: Interim Kennedy Center Director Richard Grenell, who was appointed by President Trump told Fox News Digital this week that he has a plan to get the institution out of debt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Emerging from 2024 loss, Tim Walz holds a town hall in an Iowa Republican's district
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz stood before hundreds of Iowans on Friday and admitted he doesn't have all the answers about issues facing the country. “If I did, we wouldn't be in this goddamn mess,” Walz said.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the US 'is no longer welcome' in the country
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Friday that South Africa’s ambassador to the United States “is no longer welcome” in the country, in the latest Trump administration move targeting the African nation.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rules against advocates trying to help migrants sent to Guantanamo and to stop more transfers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge ruled Friday against immigration and civil rights advocates attempting to help migrants who had been sent to the Guantanamo Bay military base — and trying to prevent further transfers — days after the Trump administration transferred all migrants out of the facility
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump environmental rollbacks would boost pollution and endanger lives, former EPA heads say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Three former Environmental Protection Agency leaders sounded an alarm Friday, saying rollbacks proposed by EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin endanger the lives of millions of Americans and abandon the agency's dual mission to protect the environment and human health.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, New York Gov. Hochul discuss Constitution gas pipeline
item.description: Trump said a pipeline would lower the region's energy prices, but it remains uncertain how it could be approved.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Mace sued for defamation over allegations in stunning floor speech
item.description: A man Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of being part of a group of “predators” in a stunning House floor speech is suing her for defamation.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats set to host town halls targeting vulnerable House Republicans
item.description: Democratic committees are set to host town halls across the country to target vulnerable House Republicans ahead of the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: DHS revokes visa of one Columbia student, arrests another pro-Palestinian protester
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) announced Friday it has revoked the visa of one Columbia University student and arrested another person involved in the pro-Palestinian protests, escalating its crackdown on those engaged in demonstrations over the conflict in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: 60 percent of voters unhappy with DOGE handling of federal workers: Poll
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE have been trying to slash their way through the federal government, offering buyouts to and laying off scores of workers. Many of their actions are now the subject of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Congress poised to force $1B cut to local DC budget, surprising many lawmakers
item.description: Congress is poised to pass a President Trump-endorsed funding bill this week that Washington, D.C., officials warn would lead to a $1 billion cut to the District’s local budget, a move that has been catching members of both parties off guard.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats struggling with unified strategy to oppose Trump, Musk
item.description: Schumer's decision to vote for a Republican-sponsored budget bill called “a huge slap in the face" for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Windsor Park deadlines loom, construction yet to start decades after homes began to sink
item.description: About $37 million is allocated to build 93 new homes for people in Windsor Park, a North Las Vegas neighborhood that began sinking decades ago. Now as deadlines are looming, lawmakers learn that construction has yet to begin.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DUI punishments higher under 2 bills introduced Monday in Nevada Senate
item.description: Republicans are proposing more severe punishment for driving under the influence with two new bills introduced Monday in the Nevada Senate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump's tariffs raise concerns of economic recession
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – President Donald Trump’s tariffs on products from Canada and Mexico have some Americans worried that they will lead the economy into a recession.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
